name: psocrmint
what_this_is: F3 -- REAL-MODE IVT interrupt delivery (INT n / IRET), the BIOS/DOS service mechanism (INT 10h/13h/16h/21h). Previously INT n in sys_mode routed ONLY to the protected-mode 8-byte IDT-gate path; pure real mode (PE=0) now delivers through the 4-byte IVT. The firmware installs an IVT entry, does INT 0x40 into a handler, and the handler IRETs back.
implementation: routing branches on real_mode (sys_mode && !CR0.PE): d_int/d_ud2 -> S_RMINT_RD (else S_INT_GATE); d_iret -> S_RMIRET (else S_IRET). S_RMINT_RD reads the 4-byte IVT entry {CS,IP} @ idt_base+vec*4; S_RMINT_PUSH does the descending 16-bit push FLAGS/CS/IP (SP-=6), loads CS (base=sel<<4) + EIP, clears IF|TF|RF|AC, retires; S_RMIRET pops IP/CS/FLAGS (SP+=6), restores the low-16 settable EFLAGS. V86 (PE=1) + protected mode keep the existing 8-byte-gate path unchanged.
non_vacuous: Asserts both traces ENTER the handler (pc=0x2a) AND RETURN via IRET (pc=0x1e).
description: Bare-metal F3 real-mode INT/IRET differential. Proves real-mode IVT interrupt delivery (push FLAGS:CS:IP, IVT vector, CS.base=sel<<4) + IRET restore byte-identical to qemu-system-i386 -cpu pentium over all 17 retired instructions, per-record. The firmware-foundation feature for INT-based BIOS services. make verify 77/77 + verify-sys 20/20 (PM/V86 delivery unaffected, real_mode=0 there).
